command.cbr.client.install.description: Install a backup client on a host.
command.cbr.client.show.description: Show backup client information by host or client ID.
command.cbr.policy.action.description: Run an action on a cloud backup policy.
command.cbr.resource.delete.description: Delete all backup resources for a protected object.
command.cbr.restore.create.description: Create a restore task from a backup snapshot.
command.cbr.service.activate.description: Activate the regional cloud backup service.
command.cbr.storage.delete.description: Cancel a backup storage repository subscription.
name: Cloud Backup and Recovery
parameter.cbr.backup-task.cancel.is_clou: Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
parameter.cbr.backup-task.cancel.source_: Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
parameter.cbr.backup-task.list.source_ty: Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
parameter.cbr.backup-task.list.status.de: Backup task status: Completed, Executing, Failed, Preparing, or Canceling
parameter.cbr.client.list.include_uninst: Whether to include hosts without an installed backup client
parameter.cbr.client.list.is_cloud.descr: Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
parameter.cbr.client.list.page_size.desc: Maximum results per page; cannot exceed 50
parameter.cbr.client.list.source_type.de: Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
parameter.cbr.client.show.instance_id.de: Backup host IDs; separate multiple IDs with ","
parameter.cbr.client.show.page_size.desc: Maximum results per page; cannot exceed 50
parameter.cbr.policy.action.action.descr: Backup policy action: 1 enables, 2 stops, and 3 runs the policy immediately
Whether an immediate policy run starts a full backup: 0 does not start one and 1 starts one
parameter.cbr.policy.action.is_cloud.des: Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
parameter.cbr.policy.create.backup_file_: Backup file JSON object: dirs and files contain at most 10 paths of at most 4095 characters; dirs is required unless all directories are selected; rule is required for advanced directory rules, where 0 includes all files, 1 includes files, and 2 excludes files
parameter.cbr.policy.create.backup_full_: Periodic full-backup JSON object: backupMode is 0 disabled or 1 enabled, and incrementNum sets the number of incremental backups between full backups
parameter.cbr.policy.create.backup_limit: Backup rate-limit JSON object: isLimitQos is 0 disabled or 1 enabled; each rateLimits item requires start in [0,23], stop in [1,24], and uploadMb in [1,9999] MB
parameter.cbr.policy.create.backup_perio: Backup schedule JSON object: rule is 0 hourly, 1 daily, 2 weekly, or 3 monthly; day is [0, 6] for weekly schedules (0 Sunday and 1-6 Monday-Saturday) or [1, 31] for monthly schedules, and is required for weekly or monthly rules; hour is [0,23] and required except for hourly rules
parameter.cbr.policy.create.backup_reten: Backup retention JSON object: type is -1 permanent, 1 days, 2 weeks, 3 months, or 4 years; value is required unless retention is permanent; hasConf enables advanced retention and conf sets day, week, month, and year counts
Backup directory rule: 0 includes all directories, 1 uses specified directories, and 2 uses advanced rules
parameter.cbr.policy.create.is_cloud.des: Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts

Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
parameter.cbr.policy.list.is_cloud.descr: Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
parameter.cbr.policy.list.policy_name.de: Backup policy name; supports fuzzy matching
parameter.cbr.policy.list.source_type.de: Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
parameter.cbr.policy.list.status.descrip: Backup policy status: CreateSuccess means active and Stop paused
parameter.cbr.policy.update.backup_file_: Backup file JSON object: dirs and files contain at most 10 paths of at most 4095 characters; dirs is required unless all directories are selected; rule is required for advanced directory rules, where 0 includes all files, 1 includes files, and 2 excludes files
parameter.cbr.policy.update.backup_full_: Periodic full-backup JSON object: backupMode is 0 disabled or 1 enabled, and incrementNum sets the number of incremental backups between full backups
parameter.cbr.policy.update.backup_limit: Backup rate-limit JSON object: isLimitQos is 0 disabled or 1 enabled; each rateLimits item requires start in [0,23], stop in [1,24], and uploadMb in [1,9999] MB
parameter.cbr.policy.update.backup_perio: Backup schedule JSON object: rule is 0 hourly, 1 daily, 2 weekly, or 3 monthly; day is [0, 6] for weekly schedules (0 Sunday and 1-6 Monday-Saturday) or [1, 31] for monthly schedules, and is required for weekly or monthly rules; hour is [0,23] and required except for hourly rules
parameter.cbr.policy.update.backup_reten: Backup retention JSON object: type is -1 permanent, 1 days, 2 weeks, 3 months, or 4 years; value is required unless retention is permanent; hasConf enables advanced retention and conf sets day, week, month, and year counts
Backup directory rule: 0 includes all directories, 1 uses specified directories, and 2 uses advanced rules

Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts

parameter.cbr.restore.cancel.source_type: Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
parameter.cbr.restore.create.existing_fi: Existing-file handling mode: 0 overwrites a same-name file, 1 skips it, and 2 retains the latest version
parameter.cbr.restore.create.file_list.d: Paths to include or exclude; required when function type is 1 or 2, with at most 10 paths
parameter.cbr.restore.create.function_ty: Restore function type: 0 includes all files, 1 restores specified paths, and 2 excludes specified paths
parameter.cbr.restore.create.include_sys: Whether an original-directory restore includes system directories
parameter.cbr.restore.create.is_cloud.de: Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
parameter.cbr.restore.create.source_type: Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
Restore target directory; omit to restore to the original directory; a missing path is created automatically
parameter.cbr.restore.create.target_inst: Target host ID; omit to restore to the original host
parameter.cbr.restore.list.is_cloud.desc: Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
parameter.cbr.restore.list.source_type.d: Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
parameter.cbr.restore.list.status.descri: Restore task status: RestoreCreating, RestoreCreateFail, RestoreCompleted, RestoreRunning, RestoreCanceling, or RestoreFail
parameter.cbr.service.activate.client_to: Client token used to make orders idempotent; must be unique within one cloud platform account
parameter.cbr.snapshot.list.is_cloud.des: Whether the protected resource is in the cloud: true for Elastic Cloud Hosts or false for local customer hosts; this parameter is scheduled for removal, so use sourceType
Backup policy name; supports fuzzy matching
Product service type: CT-ECS for Elastic Cloud Hosts, CT-DPS for bare-metal physical hosts, or CT-LS for local customer hosts
parameter.cbr.snapshot.list.status.descr: Backup snapshot status: Available means available, NotAvailable unavailable, and Restoring restoration in progress
parameter.cbr.storage.create.capacity.de: Backup storage repository capacity from 100 to 1024000 GB
parameter.cbr.storage.create.client_toke: Client token used to make orders idempotent; must be unique within one cloud platform account
parameter.cbr.storage.create.cycle_count: Subscription cycle count: 1 to 11 months or 1 to 3 years
Subscription cycle type: year or month
parameter.cbr.storage.create.multi_az.de: Whether to deploy across availability zones: 0 for a single AZ or 1 for multiple AZs; available only in supported regions
parameter.cbr.storage.create.name.descri: Backup storage repository name containing only letters, digits, and hyphens; it cannot start with a digit or hyphen or end with a hyphen
parameter.cbr.storage.delete.client_toke: Client token used to make orders idempotent; must be unique within one cloud platform account
parameter.cbr.storage.list.status.descri: Backup storage repository status: Available means available, Expire expired, Refing cancellation in progress, and Destorying destruction in progress
parameter.cbr.storage.renew.client_token: Client token used to make orders idempotent; must be unique within one cloud platform account
Renewal cycle count: 1 to 11 months or 1 to 3 years
parameter.cbr.storage.renew.cycle_type.d: Renewal cycle type: year or month
parameter.cbr.storage.resize.capacity.de: Target backup storage repository capacity from 100 to 1024000 GB
parameter.cbr.storage.resize.client_toke: Client token used to make orders idempotent; must be unique within one cloud platform account
